title: Banking institutions
description: Practice understanding of different banking institutions (commercial, traditional, credit union, online banks), pros and cons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A banking institution that might give you more money for saving and charges lower fees, but you have to qualify for membership to join.

**Which banking institution is described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A bank that has no physical branches and is only accessible through the internet or telephone. 

**Which banking institution is described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Banks that have well known names will usually have a lot of physical branch locations, but they usually pay less interest on savings. 

**Which banking institution is described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Many people choose to bank with commercial banks (also known as big banks).

**What is one good thing about using big banks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They offer the best interest rates.
They offer many different services, like accounts, loans, credit cards, and investments.
They share profits with their members.

assessment_items.question_content:
Online banks don't have physical branches, but they do have a few advantages over traditional banks. 

**What is one benefit of using an online bank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They offer a large network of ATM machines.
They re-invest profits to offset fees.

assessment_items.question_content:
A credit union offers a lot of benefits to its members. 

**What is one benefit of using a credit union?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They have branches across the country.
They offer more services than commercial banks.

assessment_items.question_content:
Commercial banks (also known as big banks) offer a variety of accounts and services, but they might not be the best fit for everyone. 

**Which of these is a disadvantage of big banks?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Their rates and fees are not the best.
They do not have many physical buildings.
They require that you use a computer or app to bank with them.
They only accept customers that live or work in a certain county.

assessment_items.question_content:
Credit Unions offer many benefits, but there are disadvantages as well. 

**Which of these represents a disadvantage of using a credit union?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Their accounts pay low interest rates.
They limit who can have an account.
They make it hard to close an account.

assessment_items.question_content:
Traditional banks do not fit everyone's needs when it comes to banking. 

**Which of these represent a con of using a traditional bank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are not personal or friendly.
They offer a limited variety of  financial services.
Their staff is more likely to recommend the wrong financial product.

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you are a young professional who has just started their career and is looking to maximize their savings. You are comfortable with technology and don't have complex banking needs that would require in-person assistance.

**Which banking institution works best for your situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As a business person, you travel to major cities regularly. Your business relies heavily on cash, so you need a bank that offers easy access to no-fee ATMs around the country. 

**Which banking institution works best for your situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are looking for a place for your elderly relative to bank. When talking to them about financial institutions, you discover they value in-person service,  a welcoming environment, and a local atmosphere.

**Which banking institution works best for this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
